name: leads-core-public-api
description: src/lib/leads (import from @/lib/leads barrel) is the Phase-2 wave-1 foundation the OTP, inquiry-submission, seller-inbox, and buyer-account builders depend on. Exports: inquirySchema (Zod v4 inquiry payload), the lead status machine (canTransition/assertTransition, LEAD_TRANSITIONS), LeadDTO + serialize(row), and newMetaEventId(). Rate limiting lives in @/lib/rate-limit and CAPTCHA in @/lib/captcha.

name: rate-limit-injectable-store
description: Redis sliding-window limiter (src/lib/rate-limit) depends on a narrow RateLimitRedis interface (zremrangebyscore/zadd/zcard/pexpire/zrangeWithScoresAsc), NOT ioredis directly. Production uses ioRedisStore(getRedisConnection()); unit tests inject an in-memory fake so no network/ephemeral Redis is needed. limit() runs the commands sequentially (not MULTI) — safe because each call awaits its own zadd before its own zcard, so it can never under-count and wrongly admit. Fails CLOSED by default on a Redis outage (lead submission), fail-open is opt-in.

title: Lead status machine models the lost sink with a multi-source transition
rationale: The listings machine gives each named action a single from→to edge. Leads deviate: LEAD_TRANSITIONS.from is a readonly LeadStatus[] so the single 'lose' action fires from new/contacted/negotiating. Rationale: 'lost' is a sink reachable from every live stage; enumerating lose_new/lose_contacted/lose_negotiating would be noise. Forward progress stays strictly one-step (no skip to won, no reopening from won/lost). ALLOWED adjacency and transitionAction() are still derived from the table, preserving the listings pattern's single-source-of-truth guarantee.

content: CAPTCHA verify (src/lib/captcha, Cloudflare Turnstile) is env-guarded exactly like email (Resend) and phone-OTP (Twilio): with no TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY it no-ops to success:true skipped:true so dev/test never hit the network; with a secret it verifies and FAILS CLOSED (success:false) on any network/parse error or non-OK HTTP. Server helper uses TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY (never logged); the public NEXT_PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY belongs to the wave-2 client widget. Neither env var is in .env.example yet — add before enabling in prod.
